pr.title: feat(live-logs): Phase 3 SSE real-time log streaming backend

comments.body: You have reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews. You can see your limits in the [Codex usage dashboard](https://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/usage).

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

There's a memory leak here. The `_histories[run_id]` list is never cleared, so `not self._histories[run_id]` is always `False` after the first event is published. Consequently, the entries for `run_id` in `_channels` and `_histories` are never removed, even after a run is complete. This will cause memory to grow indefinitely as more runs are processed.

A mechanism to explicitly clean up resources for a completed run should be introduced. For example, you could add a `close(run_id)` method to the publisher that can be called by the agent supervisor when a run terminates. This method would be responsible for removing the `run_id` entries from `_channels` and `_histories`.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Using `list.pop(0)` to maintain a bounded history is inefficient, with O(n) complexity where n is the size of the history. For a large `max_history` (default 5000), this can become a performance bottleneck if events are published frequently.

Consider changing `_histories` to store `collections.deque` objects with a `maxlen`. This would make appending to the history an O(1) operation and automatically handle eviction, removing the need for this manual check and `pop(0)` call.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

There's a small discrepancy between the data model documentation and the implementation. Here, the enum values for `stream` are listed as `out, err, system`, but the implementation in `moonmind/services/observability/models.py` uses `stdout, stderr, system`. For clarity and consistency, the documentation should match the implementation.

```suggestion
- `stream` (Enum: stdout, stderr, system): The source of the content (stdout, stderr, or MoonMind supervisor).
```

## Code Review

This pull request implements Phase 3 of the live logs feature, introducing a Server-Sent Events (SSE) endpoint for real-time log streaming of active task runs. It includes a new observability service with a pub/sub publisher, an SSE generator, and comprehensive integration tests. Feedback highlights a potential memory leak in the publisher's history management, suggests using collections.deque for more efficient history buffering, and notes a naming discrepancy between the documentation and the implementation of log stream types.

## Empty except 'except' clause does nothing but pass and there is no explanatory comment. ---

In general, to fix an empty except without changing functionality, you either (a) add a clarifying comment if truly intentional, and/or (b) add minimal logging or metrics while still not re-raising the exception. This preserves behavior (exceptions are still swallowed) but makes the decision explicit and observable.

For this specific case in moonmind/services/observability/publisher.py, the best fix is:

  • Keep the behavior of not propagating asyncio.QueueFull (still dropping the event).
  • Add a short comment explaining that events are intentionally dropped when a subscriber’s queue is full, to avoid blocking the publisher.
  • Optionally add a very lightweight debug-level log for diagnostic purposes. However, adding logging would require importing logging and might be considered a behavior change (new log noise). To stay maximally conservative, we can start with a clear comment only, which directly satisfies the CodeQL rule (“or has an explanatory comment”).

Concretely:

  • In publish, lines 35–39: replace the bare pass under except asyncio.QueueFull: with a comment like # Intentionally drop event for slow subscriber when queue is full to avoid blocking..
  • No additional imports or method definitions are required if we only add a comment.

## Empty except 'except' clause does nothing but pass and there is no explanatory comment. --- To fix this kind of issue, you either (1) remove the unnecessary try/except and let the exception propagate, (2) handle the exception meaningfully, or (3) if it is genuinely expected and safe to ignore, add a clear comment so that the empty except is intentional and comprehensible.

Here, the test is simply iterating an async generator; async for will consume it until completion and normally handle StopAsyncIteration internally. Manually catching StopAsyncIteration around the whole loop is unnecessary. The cleanest fix that preserves behavior is to remove the except StopAsyncIteration: pass block entirely and keep the finally: await gen.aclose() for cleanup. That way, if a StopAsyncIteration escapes unnaturally (e.g., from inside the loop body), the test will fail, which is desirable. Concretely, in tests/integration/api/test_live_logs.py, replace the try/except/finally structure in test_disconnect_cleanup_releases_subscriber so it only has try: ... async for ... and finally: await gen.aclose() with no except block. No new imports or helper methods are required.

## Unused import Import of 'uuid' is not used. --- To fix the problem, remove the unused import of uuid from moonmind/services/observability/publisher.py. In general, unused imports should be deleted to keep the code clean and avoid misleading future readers into thinking the module is used.

Concretely, in moonmind/services/observability/publisher.py, edit the top of the file so that line 2 (import uuid) is removed, leaving the other imports (asyncio and from typing import ...) intact. No additional methods, imports, or definitions are needed; we are only deleting an unnecessary line.

## Unused import Import of 'LogStreamEvent' is not used. ---

To fix an unused import, you remove the import statement (or the specific unused name) so that only actually used symbols are imported. This eliminates unnecessary dependencies and satisfies the static analysis rule.

Concretely, in moonmind/services/observability/subscriber.py, delete the line that imports LogStreamEvent, since the function log_stream_generator does not use it. No other code changes are required: the rest of the file already functions by using event objects yielded from pub.subscribe without explicitly referencing LogStreamEvent.

This means:

  • Edit moonmind/services/observability/subscriber.py.
  • Remove line 6: from moonmind.services.observability.models import LogStreamEvent.
  • No new methods, imports, or definitions are needed.

Terminal-status detection for 410 uses `"cancelled"`, but `AgentRunState` uses `"canceled"` (single-L) and also includes other terminal states like `timed_out`. As written, canceled/timed_out runs will incorrectly continue streaming; consider using `is_terminal_agent_run_state(record.status)` or `TERMINAL_AGENT_RUN_STATES` to decide when to return 410.
```suggestion
    if getattr(record, "status", None) in ["completed", "failed", "canceled", "cancelled", "timed_out"]:
```

`FileResponse` is imported twice (once on the earlier import block and again here alongside `StreamingResponse`). This redundancy typically fails linting (unused/duplicate import); consolidate into a single `from fastapi.responses import FileResponse, StreamingResponse` import.

Task T014 mentions running `flake8`/`mypy`, but this repo is configured for Black + Ruff (and no mypy config is present). Consider updating this step to reference the actual lint/type-check tooling used here (e.g., `ruff` / `black`).
```suggestion
- [x] T014 Run `ruff` and `black` against `moonmind/services/observability` and `moonmind/api/routers/task_runs.py`
```

This test uses `_AlwaysDisconnected()` when creating the generator. Since `log_stream_generator` checks `request.is_disconnected()` before yielding, it will break immediately and may never register a live subscriber queue—so the assertion can pass even if real subscriber cleanup is broken. Use a request stub that disconnects after at least one chunk is yielded (or after live subscription starts).
```suggestion
    class _DisconnectAfterFirstCheck:
        """Request stub that is connected for the first check, then disconnects."""

        def __init__(self) -> None:
            self._checks = 0

        async def is_disconnected(self) -> bool:
            self._checks += 1
            return self._checks > 1

    # Pre-publish one event so history is non-empty — the generator should
    # register a live subscriber and yield at least one chunk before this
    # request starts reporting as disconnected.
    await pub.publish(run_id, _evt(0))

    gen = log_stream_generator(run_id, _DisconnectAfterFirstCheck(), since=0, publisher=pub)

    # Collect the first SSE chunk(s). After the first disconnect check, the
    # stub starts reporting disconnected, causing the generator to break.
```

The DOC-REQ coverage list claims this file validates the `/api/task-runs/{id}/logs/stream` endpoint contract, but the tests only exercise the publisher/generator directly (no FastAPI client or route-level assertions). Either add API-level tests for the route/headers, or adjust the stated coverage.
```suggestion
DOC-REQ coverage (publisher/subscriber layer only):
```

The DOC-REQ coverage list claims 410 fallback coverage (DOC-REQ-007), but there’s no test asserting the router returns HTTP 410 for terminal runs. Either add a route-level test for the 410 behavior or remove DOC-REQ-007 from the coverage list.
```suggestion

```

`/logs/stream` streams from the in-process global `observability.publisher`, but there’s currently no code in the repo that calls `publisher.publish(...)` (search only finds definitions/tests). As a result, the SSE endpoint will never emit live events in production. The PR likely needs wiring from the log-producing runtime (e.g., supervisor/output parser) into `publisher.publish` (or a Redis-backed implementation) so events are actually pushed.

The OpenAPI `responses` only documents 404, but this handler also returns 403 (non-superuser) and 410 (terminal run fallback). Adding these to `responses={...}` will keep the generated API contract accurate.

`uuid` is imported but unused in this module. Please remove it to avoid failing Ruff/flake8 unused-import checks.
```suggestion

```

`LogStreamEvent` is imported but unused in this module. Please remove it to keep lint clean.
```suggestion

```

PEP8/Black expects two blank lines between top-level imports and `class` declarations. Adding the blank line(s) here will avoid style/lint failures.
```suggestion

```

`publish()` iterates directly over `self._channels[run_id]` while `subscribe()` can remove queues in `finally`. Mutating a list during iteration can skip subscribers. Iterate over a shallow copy (e.g., `list(self._channels[run_id])`) and/or guard channel mutations with an `asyncio.Lock`.

Per-run history is never evicted: `_histories[run_id]` is never removed, so once a run has any published logs its entry will stay in memory indefinitely. Consider purging histories when a run is terminal/inactive, or adding a TTL/explicit `close_run()` API to reclaim memory.
```suggestion
            channels = self._channels.get(run_id)
            if channels is not None and q in channels:
                channels.remove(q)
            # If no more subscribers remain for this run, drop its history and channel state
            if not self._channels.get(run_id):
                self._channels.pop(run_id, None)
                self._histories.pop(run_id, None)
```

This plan references `moonmind/api/routers/task_runs.py`, but the actual API router lives at `api_service/api/routers/task_runs.py`. Updating the path here will keep the project-structure section accurate.

`since` is accepted as an arbitrary integer; negative values don’t make sense for sequence resumption and could lead to surprising history replays. Consider validating it with `Query(ge=0)` (and potentially an upper bound) to make the API contract explicit.

Several tasks reference `moonmind/api/routers/task_runs.py`, but the actual router file in this repo is under `api_service/api/routers/task_runs.py`. Updating these paths will prevent confusion for future implementers/readers.

## Pull request overview Adds the backend building blocks for Phase 3 “live logs” via Server-Sent Events (SSE), introducing an in-memory pub/sub publisher + SSE generator and exposing a new `/task-runs/{id}/logs/stream` route, alongside supporting specs and tests. **Changes:** - Introduces `moonmind/services/observability/` with `LogStreamEvent`, an in-memory `ObservabilityPublisher`, and an SSE `log_stream_generator`. - Adds `GET /task-runs/{id}/logs/stream` in `api_service/api/routers/task_runs.py` using `StreamingResponse`. - Adds Phase 3 documentation/spec artifacts and a new async test module for publisher/generator behavior. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 13 out of 15 changed files in this pull request and generated 16 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | var/pr_resolver/attempts/finalize_attempt_3.json | Adds PR resolver attempt artifact metadata. | | tests/integration/api/test_live_logs.py | Adds async tests for publisher fan-out/history and SSE formatting. | | specs/112-live-logs-phase-3/tasks.md | Task checklist + implementation notes for Phase 3. | | specs/112-live-logs-phase-3/spec.md | Feature spec and requirements for Phase 3 live logs. | | specs/112-live-logs-phase-3/research.md | Research notes / transport decision record. | | specs/112-live-logs-phase-3/quickstart.md | Quickstart instructions for running tests / manual SSE checks. | | specs/112-live-logs-phase-3/plan.md | Implementation plan and project-structure outline. | | specs/112-live-logs-phase-3/data-model.md | Documents the live-log event data model. | | specs/112-live-logs-phase-3/contracts/requirements-traceability.md | Requirements-to-validation traceability matrix. | | moonmind/services/observability/subscriber.py | SSE generator bridging publisher events to `text/event-stream`. | | moonmind/services/observability/publisher.py | In-memory pub/sub with bounded history + `since=` replay. | | moonmind/services/observability/models.py | Pydantic DTO + stream enum for emitted log events. | | moonmind/services/observability/__init__.py | Declares the new observability package. | | moonmind/services/__init__.py | Ensures `moonmind.services` is a package. | | api_service/api/routers/task_runs.py | Adds SSE endpoint and required imports/response handling. |
